explanations.1: This tells Docker to use a specific syntax version for parsing the Dockerfile, which enables newer features like the cache mount on line 19.
explanations.12: These environment variables tell Python not to create .pyc files and to output logs immediately instead of buffering them, which is useful for seeing real-time output in containers.
explanations.19: This creates a temporary cache folder that persists between builds so apt-get doesn't re-download the same packages every time you rebuild the image.
explanations.39: This checks if a user with that name already exists before trying to create it, preventing errors if the image was already set up.
explanations.69: This checks if the welcome message marker already exists in the file, so it only adds the message once even if you rebuild the container multiple times.
explanations.89: Like line 19, this caches pip's downloaded packages between builds to speed up dependency installation on subsequent rebuilds.
